From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Pakistani badminton player

Mahoor Shahzad

| Personal information | |
| --- | --- |
| Born | (1996-10-17) 17 October 1996 (age 29)  [Karachi](/wiki/Karachi "Karachi"), Pakistan |
| Years active | 2014–present |

| Weight | 56 kg (123 lb) |
| Sport | |

|  | |

| Current ranking | 175 (WS) 248 (WD) (26 July 2022) |
| [BWF profile](https://bwfbadminton.com/player/51218) | |
|  | |
| Medal record  |  |  | | --- | --- | |  | | | Women's [badminton](/wiki/Badminton "Badminton") | | | | Representing  [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan "Pakistan") | | | | [South Asian Games](/wiki/Badminton_at_the_South_Asian_Games "Badminton at the South Asian Games") | | | | Bronze medal – third place | [2019 Kathmandu–Pokhara](/wiki/Badminton_at_the_2019_South_Asian_Games "Badminton at the 2019 South Asian Games") | Women's team | | |

**Mahoor Shahzad** (born 17 October 1996) is a Pakistani [badminton](/wiki/Badminton "Badminton") player.[[1]](#cite_note-1) She has competed at the [2014 Asian Games](/wiki/Badminton_at_the_2014_Asian_Games "Badminton at the 2014 Asian Games"),[[2]](#cite_note-2) and also at the [2018](/wiki/Badminton_at_the_2018_Commonwealth_Games "Badminton at the 2018 Commonwealth Games") and [2022 Commonwealth Games](/wiki/Badminton_at_the_2022_Commonwealth_Games "Badminton at the 2022 Commonwealth Games").[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)

She represented [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan_at_the_2020_Summer_Olympics "Pakistan at the 2020 Summer Olympics") at the [2020 Summer Olympics](/wiki/Badminton_at_the_2020_Summer_Olympics "Badminton at the 2020 Summer Olympics") in Tokyo after receiving a [tripartite invitation](/wiki/Tripartite_Commission "Tripartite Commission"), becoming the first Pakistani badminton player to compete at the [Olympic Games](/wiki/Badminton_at_the_Summer_Olympics "Badminton at the Summer Olympics").[[5]](#cite_note-:0-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6) She was also Pakistan's flag bearer at the [opening ceremony](/wiki/2020_Summer_Olympics_opening_ceremony "2020 Summer Olympics opening ceremony") along with the sports shooter [Muhammad Khalil Akhtar](/wiki/Muhammad_Khalil_Akhtar "Muhammad Khalil Akhtar").[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8)

## Education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mahoor_Shahzad&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Education")]

Shahzad graduated from the [Institute of Business Administration, Karachi](/wiki/Institute_of_Business_Administration,_Karachi "Institute of Business Administration, Karachi") in 2018, with Economics and Mathematics as her subjects.

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mahoor_Shahzad&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

Shahzad began playing badminton in 2008 in Karachi.[[9]](#cite_note-9) She is a six-time national badminton champion of Pakistan.[[10]](#cite_note-10) She won the women's singles titles at the 2017[[11]](#cite_note-11) and 2019[[12]](#cite_note-12) editions of the [Pakistan International Series](/wiki/Pakistan_International_(badminton) "Pakistan International (badminton)").

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mahoor_Shahzad&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Personal life")]

Shahzad's father, Muhammad Shahzad, is an [indoor rower](/wiki/Indoor_rowing "Indoor rowing") athlete. Her sister, Rabia Shahzad, is a [weightlifter](/wiki/Olympic_weightlifting "Olympic weightlifting") who has won several international medals for Pakistan.[[13]](#cite_note-13)[[14]](#cite_note-14)[[15]](#cite_note-15)[[16]](#cite_note-16)
